item.title: GeForce NOW Adds Another Seven Games Headlined by DON’T NOD’s Aphelion and Super Meat Boy 3D Amidst Summer Sale Drive
item.content:encoded:
NVIDIA's cloud streaming GeForce NOW service has just revealed the next seven games supported by the cloud service, which brings all of your games across multiple storefronts into one place. This latest batch adds titles like Super Meat Boy 3D and Aphelion, though just as significant as the new games entering the service is the fact that the GeForce NOW summer sale means it's the best time to subscribe if you haven't already.

Starting with the games, though there are technically seven titles now supported by the cloud service, one of this week's entries is only a demo, with the full game currently missing a release date. That game is Embers of the Uncrowned, developed and published by Nexon.

Though the full release is still a ways off, the isometric dark fantasy game already looks impressive with its high graphical fidelity and detailed environments, all of which can shine even further when you're playing through GeForce NOW, unburdened by the limits of your own hardware.

While DON'T NOD's latest Aphelion and Team Meat's 3D evolution of the 2D platforming sensation are two of the more notable recent releases, they're not the only notable titles in this week's batch. Citizen Sleeper, which will be available for free for players to add to their library on the Epic Games Store beginning today until June 25, is also worth recognizing.

Particularly since it was recently removed from Xbox Game Pass, so if you need a new way to play it, EGS and GeForce NOW have you covered. Here's the full list of games joining the library of games supported by the cloud streaming service:

  • Embers of the Uncrowned Demo (New release on Steam, available 13)
  • Pro Cycling Manager 26 (New release on Steam, available June 15)
  • Aphelion (Steam)
  • Citizen Sleeper (Epic Game Store, Free from June 18-25)
  • Megastore Simulator (Steam)
  • OPERATOR (Steam)
  • Super Meat Boy 3D (Xbox, available on Game Pass)

As for the GeForce summer sale, if you've been considering subscribing to GeForce NOW, there's really no time like the present, since players going for the 12-month Performance tier membership can get $35 off the total price and those leaping for the 12-month Ultimate tier membership get a whopping $70 off.

"Performance memberships deliver high-quality cloud gaming across devices," NVIDIA writes, "while Ultimate unlocks GeForce RTX 5080-class performance in the cloud with up to 5K resolution, up to 120 frames per second and advanced technologies like NVIDIA DLSS, ray tracing and NVIDIA Reflex."

With how much more difficult and expensive it is getting to upgrade your hardware, cloud services like GeForce NOW are getting more difficult to resist if you're someone who has been hesitant about cloud gaming. If anything, it's at the very least worth a try when you're getting a discount.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.

item.title: Warren Spector-helmed Thick as Thieves developers OtherSide lay off 17 staff as they cancel multiplayer immersive sim Argos: Riders on the Storm
item.content:encoded:
Argos "could have been a huge success in normal times", the studio claim

Thick As Thieves developers OtherSide Entertainment have laid off 17 employees following the cancellation of a multiplayer immersive sim dubbed Argos: Riders on the Storm. The studio's helmed by Looking Glass Studios veterans Warren Spector and Paul Neurath - key figures in shaping the likes of Deus Ex and System Shock 2 - said via a spokesperson that they believe Argos "could have been a huge success in normal times".

In a statement to Game Developer, the OtherSide spokesperson said the 17 staff were let go "effective end of May" following the call to can Argos. Notably, the studio's other game, the stealthy Thick as Thieves launched on May 20th after what certainly looked a fairly last minute switch of focus from PvPvE to solo and co-op play.

As for Argos, it "could have been a huge success in normal times" the spokesperson reckoned, but the "brutally challenging" state of the games industry at the moment has left soldiering on with it "unviable for now". "To any studios out there hiring, we cannot recommend these impacted people highly enough," they added regarding the staff let go.

Gauging whether that assessment of Argos' chances feels right is made rather tough by the fact the details OtherSide revealed about it make for relatively slim pickings. "From the creative mind of Warren Spector comes a new multiplayer Immersive Simulation, letting players 'pick a playstyle' in an evolving alien and oddly familiar world," read the game's brief blurb on the studio's website, which has now been scrubbed away to leave Thick As Thieves standing alone. Also absent from that page is Underworld Ascendant, OtherSide's crowdfunded spiritual successor to Ultima Underworld which sadly released in a rather broken state and saw its review verdicts suffer accordingly.

Solidarity and best of luck the the workers affected by these layoffs at OtherSide.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/warren-spector-helmed-thick-as-thieves-developers-otherside-lay-off-17-staff-as-they-cancel-multiplayer-immersive-sim-argos-riders-on-the-storm

item.title: Listen To Overwatch’s Best Voice Line Of All Time (Volume Warning)
item.content:encoded:
Overwatch’s new season kicked off this week with the addition of Shion, a new damage hero and an Omnic member of the Hashimoto clan. She is the most cyberpunk character in the shooter, and her playstyle is erratic and chaotic. This is usually juxtaposed by her calm, collected, but cruel demeanor. In most of her voice line interactions she is in control, taunting other characters and maintaining the upper hand. When does this woman let loose? When Ana Nano Boosts her.

Ana’s ultimate, Nano Boost, is a support ability that powers up the recipient’s damage output, reduces their damage taken, and gives them a strong dose of healing. The quick energy boost makes every hero who receives it shout something, whether it be warning their enemies of their newfound power or really playing up the character’s personality. Ranking these voice lines has become a popular trend for Overwatch fans, and now I imagine several creators will be updating their lists because Shion’s is one of the best.

SHION’S NANO VOICELINE IS SOOO GOODDD OH MYYY pic.twitter.com/7hqaKXXhit

— shade 🤍 SHION!! (@sh4despears) June 16, 2026

My girl is shrieking. The wordless yell would no doubt instill fear in anyone on the wrong side of her pistols or the motorcycle she rides and then sends barreling down the choke point. Shion’s violent nature is always under the surface of even the most polite conversations she has with other heroes, but her human-like mask slips in battle, especially when she’s powered up.

I was not initially a fan of Shion’s design as I was disappointed that Overwatch was humanizing a synthetic Omnic character to give her the same “conventionally attractive” look the game has been criticized for falling back on over the years, but I am compelled by the bits of character exploration we’ve gotten illuminating how she intentionally made herself look human, much to the chagrin of Ramattra, who has been trying to lead Omnics to fight against human oppression. 



https://kotaku.com/overwatch-shion-new-damage-hero-voice-lines-nano-boost-2000708080

item.title: Control Resonant Is Looking To ‘Attract The Action RPG Crowd’
item.content:encoded:
Control Resonant is taking some pretty big swings for a sequel, with numerous aspects from the original being heavily altered. Not only is there a new protagonist and setting, but Resonant is breaking into an entirely different genre, with guns being ditched for melee action instead.

It’s a bold step in a new direction, especially for a studio that’s practically mastered the art of the third-person shooter, but could it come with the risk of alienating the original fanbase? Remedy doesn’t seem to think so; instead, it’s seen as an opportunity to reach an entirely new audience—namely, the action RPG crowd.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Resonant Is All About Introducing More People To Control

Speaking with the Art Director for Control Resonant, Elmeri Raitanen, we discussed the risks and rewards of the bold shift from third-person shooter to melee-action brawler.

When exploring why Remedy chose to make this change, Raitanen settled on a handful of reasons.

Firstly, it made sense for the narrative. It was always the plan for the sequel to be Dylan’s story, and with numerous themes of juxtaposition and contrast between the Faden siblings, it was decided very early on that he would serve as the ‘brawler who gets up close and personal,’ compared to Jesse’s more ranged approach.

  • Related: Future Control Titles Depend On If Resonant ‘Does Well’

However, a new genre also meant an opportunity to reach a wider player base, and Remedy seems keen to do so. Raitanen explained that fans who might prefer action RPGs didn’t give Control a go, and that’s a ‘massive player base who might have enjoyed Control if it was a little more in their wheelhouse.’

With that in mind, there’s hope that Resonant could ‘attract a little more of that action RPG crowd,’ with more eyes on the game the better. That did prompt me to question whether there’s a risk that, with a new genre, could fans of the original potentially be alienated by this new experience? Raitanen was encouraging in his response:

We have been thinking about it, of course. I could still say that, regardless of the many things that we have changed, there is still a strong undercurrent of the original vibes of Control in this one as well.

It’s undoubtedly a huge shift, but having played the game for myself, I can verify that Resonant still feels like Control, and judging by the combat so far, you wouldn’t think this is Remedy’s first foray into the melee-action genre.

Still, it’s a topic worth discussing. How do you feel about Control Resonant shifting genres? Do you wish it remained a third-person shooter, or are you excited to give the melee action a go? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Destiny 2’s final update not moving the needle at Bungie, and don’t forget to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: All Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
A fresh week in Forza Horizon 6 means a new slew of content to churn through, and the rewards for the next month during Series 2, Horizon Decades, begin with a return to Summer.

Festival Playlists in Forza Horizon 6 are spread across four weeks, each featuring a different season with map changes and challenges, before moving on to the next playlist. Now, Horizon Decades kicks off with Summer.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Daily Challenges & Rewards

As usual, Daily Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 drop on a daily basis, but they remain available until the end of the current season, and potentially even longer.

This means that you don’t have to make sure you log into the game daily to tick off the objectives, and can instead stack them up to create a to-do list to tick off before the end of the season.

  • Related: Forza Horizon 6 Giving All Players a Free Car After Eliminator Issues

The seven Daily Challenges for the Summer season in the Horizon Decades Festival Playlist and the rewards they provide are outlined in the table below.

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Weekly Challenges & Rewards

As ever, Weekly Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 are themed around a specific vehicle, as this week’s car of choice is the DeLorean DMC-12.

You’ll need to own the car to tick through the challenges, which must be completed in the set order, and the final reward is Five Festival Points and 25,000 Credits. The good news, however, is that you can earn the car by winning a Seasonal Championship.

Each step of the Weekly Challenge in the Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist is detailed below.

  1. Own and drive the DeLorean DMC-12.
  2. Maintain a speed of 88mph for 10 seconds.
  3. Take a photo of the DeLorean DMC-12.
  4. Win a Road Race in the DeLorean DMC-12

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Events & Rewards

The table below highlights all other Seasonal events and challenges in the Welcome to Japan Spring Festival Playlist, and all the available rewards.

Check out the Horizon Decades Series trailer below.

What do you make of the rewards available this week? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at all the Wheelspin Exclusive cars in Forza Horizon 6, and don’t forget to sign up

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Roadmap Details Huge 1.1.0 Patch
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has published an all-new roadmap for Escape from Tarkov, uncovering everything being added or changed in the stellar extraction shooter over the next few months. It includes an in-game event in July called Blackout, and then a massive 1.1.0 patch that introduces the game’s first season as part of a fresh, exciting system.

The team also revealed what’s coming beyond that monumental update, including a rework to Lighthouse, which has been teased in recent weeks, the touted GPS mechanic, and the deployment of Unity 6.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Tarkov Has Massive Changes Coming

In the recent post from Battlestate Games, it was revealed that the remainder of June will yield ‘regular mini events’, and then in July, an event called Blackout will hit the platform.

Also in July comes the 1.1.0 update, an enormous patch with stacks of content and changes:

  • Seasons system and Season 1 launch
  • Technical changes focused on game stability and performance
  • Reworked task system
  • Group shared kill objectives for tasks
  • Rebalanced insurance system
  • Rebalanced hideout progression
  • Improved beginner assistance packs
  • Streets of Tarkov optimization
  • AI behavior improvements and fixes
  • Expanded interactive mechanics on locations
  • New weapons and gear
  • New PMC customization options
  • Observer camera
  • Additional anti-cheat measures

If that wasn’t enough, Q3 2026 is set to bring about further amendments to the extraction ecosystem, including another in-game event and a much-needed rework of Lighthouse, arguably the most hated Escape from Tarkov map.

There will also be a skip Prestige requirements function added, but it’ll cost a ‘large in-game fee.’

  • RELATED: Get The Latest Escape from Tarkov Codes

New players will also be getting the simplified GPS mechanic that helps them navigate the game’s complex maps, and the extraction systems will be getting reworked.

Additionally, the trader system, loot pool, reputation system, and raid reconnect function will all be rebalanced or adjusted in some way.

For the tech-savvy folks out there, Q3 2026 will also bring about Unity 6 and FSR 4.0 for engine, graphical, and performance-based boosts.

Are you excited to see what comes this year for Escape from Tarkov? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that GTA 6’s cover art has been revealed, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: GTA 6 Cover Art and Pre-Order Reveal Comes With a Fresh Look at Vice City
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar didn’t just reveal the GTA 6 cover art and the date pre-orders go live, it also added a new image to its official website offering a fresh look at Vice City.

The impressive dynamic image can be seen in motion as you scroll down the GTA 6 landing page. It shows Vice City at sunset with various vehicles flying overhead, cars driving on the freeway, and advertisements at work. We also see a ferris wheel in motion, ships moving on the water, and a close look at what look like high-rise apartment buildings set within construction work.

This fresh look at Vice City shows just how alive Rockstar’s virtual world is, even when viewed from a great distance.

As you'd expect, fans are already scanning the image for hints at what to expect from GTA 6 when it launches this November. Apart from marveling at the realism on display, fans are trying to work out exactly which part of Vice City we're seeing here. They're counting the vehicles in air, on sea, and on land, and wondering if we'll get to actually pilot and drive them (we can see a yacht and container ship moving). They're also taking a close look at the windows to see if there are any clues not just to what may be inside, but Rockstar's approach to building interiors with GTA 6. Some are suggesting we're technically getting new gameplay footage here, although that's a bit of a stretch.

We also have new Lucia and Jason artwork, which represents a stylized take on the two GTA 6 protagonists.

It's also worth noting that Rockstar has finally updated its pinned tweet, which had still pointed to GTA 6 Trailer 2 and what was then a May 26, 2026 release date. Rockstar has also changed the color scheme of its official logo from yellow to pink, reflecting the tone of GTA 6 itself.

There are of course a number of unanswered questions around GTA 6. With pre-orders going live on June 25, we'll find out the price of the game. Will Rockstar stick with $70, or go higher, potentially setting a new benchmark for the video game industry? And what about the performance of the game itself? Will Rockstar reveal the frame-rate and resolution of GTA 6 across the PlayStation 5, PlayStation 5 Pro, Xbox Series X, and Xbox Series S?

GTA 6 Vice City Screenshots

GTA 6 launches on November 19, 2026 on PS5 and Xbox Series X and S.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

item.title: GTA 6 Cover Artwork Revealed, Pre-Orders Begin June 25
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar has just revealed the cover artwork for GTA 6, and announced that pre-orders for the long-awaited blockbuster will finally begin on June 25.

The game's artwork was revealed this morning in a brief 30-second teaser video that's now live on Rockstar's YouTube channel, or you can watch it right below. To be clear, this is not the game's next trailer — we're still waiting for that to land at some point. Maybe June 25 is the date for that, too?

Regardless, with fans able to spend money on pre-ordering the game next week, June 25 will be the date we finally discover GTA 6's price point — something that has been the subject of intense speculation over the past year or so. Will the game launch at $70? $80? Some estimates have suggested it could arrive at as much as $100 to cover the cost of its mammoth development — though the game will surely earn this back over time, in a similar manner to how the hugely-lucrative GTA 5 continues to rake in money even now.

Speaking at the video game executive conference iicon in April this year, Strauss Zelnick, the boss of GTA 6 publisher Take-Two Interactive, suggested that his company would keep the game's cost in check to ensure players felt the price was "fair."

"Consumers pay for the value that you bring to them, and our job is to charge way way way less of the value delivery," Zelnick said. "How you feel about something you buy is the intersection of the thing itself and what you pay for. Consumers need to feel like the thing itself is amazing and the price they were charged was fair for what they got.

"What we think about is making the most spectacular piece of entertainment on Earth, in history – and it’s a pretty daunting challenge," Zelnick continued. "If we do that, and if we’re of service to our customers, then the upside will take care of itself.”

Now, let's take a look at that cover art in more detail, via this high-quality version that Rockstar has just shared with IGN:

The image is of course headlined by GTA 6's dual protagonists, Lucia Caminos and Jason Duval, sandwiched between a motorbike riding pulling off a trick alongside a cop car, and a helicopter with a minigun strapped to one side.

Lower down the cover we see an unknown woman wearing a necklace showing the word "siempre" (meaning "always"). On the right, one of the game's previously-announced supporting characters is featured: Boobie Ike, a real estate mogul and club owner.

At the base of the cover it looks like we can see Raul Bautista, a veteran Vice City bank robber who crosses paths with Lucia and Jason, alongside a sports car, a speedboat (and flamingo), plus a hungry-looking alligator.

The image seeks to establish the game's core concepts and characters: being able to tote guns and drive all sorts of vehicles across an urban environment, but also head out into the open world, where wild animals roam.

For more on what could the biggest video game launch in history, check out the latest on the stolen data from the Rockstar hack saga and Zelnick's comments on GTA 6 being a digital-only release. GTA 6 is set to launch on November 19, 2026 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S, and we'll be keeping up with everything leading up its release.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

item.title: Amid Concern Over Xbox Studio 'Bloodbath,' New Report Says Developers Are Being 'Punished Today for Following Orders'
item.content:encoded:
"It's going to be bad."

A new report suggests Xbox layoffs will likely result in a "bloodbath" of job losses and studio closures, leaving developers "punished" simply for following Microsoft's orders.

Word of Xbox staff redundancies and development studio shutdowns is now rife, following an ominous warning from newly-installed Xbox CEO Asha Sharma that the console maker's slim profit margin was no longer sustainable. While it has been reported that Xbox studios such as South of Midnight maker Compulsion Games, Kiln and Keeper developer Double Fine, and Hellblade helmer Ninja Theory are just some of the teams now facing shutdown, the exact details remain unclear and unfinalized.

Microsoft is believed to be timing the layoffs with the end of its financial year on June 30, with talks ongoing among studio bosses over which companies could be sold off, spun out and kept open independently — even if this still results in numerous job losses. IGN has asked Microsoft for comment but has yet to receive a response.

The 100 Best Xbox Games of All Time

Now, in a new YouTube report from Bloomberg's Jason Schreier, the veteran reporter claimed that "the word bloodbath has been thrown around among people I talk to who know about what's going to happen. It's going to be bad." Schreier's report also delves into the background of why these layoffs are happening now, and suggests that Xbox teams have struggled to adapt as Microsoft's attitudes to gaming and demands of its development teams have shifted over the years.

Long-term, Xbox never really recovered from the Xbox One era, Schreier argued. The Xbox Series X/S has failed to gain back ground in the console war against PlayStation, while the growth of Xbox Game Pass has plateaued. The past decade has seen Microsoft go on a spending spree to gain Game Pass content, buying the very studios that the company now seeks to shut down or divest.

Most notable was the $68.7 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard, devised by former Xbox boss Phil Spencer during the tail end of the Covid-era gaming boom, but already looking less attractive by the time its lengthy buyout process closed due to the enormous costs involved and a changed economic and strategic landscape.

Every Video Game Franchise Xbox Owns After Acquiring Activision Blizzard

The past two years have seen the last of the Covid growth era evaporate, Roblox blow up as a major drain on player attention and spending, and AI became a major new focus for tech companies such as Microsoft. These issues all likely contributed to the need for Xbox to meet tougher financial targets from executives, resulting in pressure to cut costs.

"That's when we see things start to get really bad," Schreier said. "We see four mass layoffs in two years, the closure of studios under the Xbox umbrella like Arkane Austin and Tango Gameworks, which wound up going to Krafton which saved that studio, and the cancellation of projects like Everwild, Perfect Dark, and Project Blackbird."

This period was marked by political in-fighting at Xbox as different divisions and executives struggled to gain priority. This also resulted in it becoming far more difficult to get new gaming projects greenlit, and Xbox's multiplatform push to boost profits that saw more games launch for PlayStation 5 and Nintendo Switch.

"The last couple of years at Xbox have been quite messy, people start openly questioning Game Pass and saying it's cannibalizing sales," Schreier continued, referencing the hundreds of millions of dollars reportedly lost by putting Call of Duty in the subscription. "What might be good for the Game Pass folks may not be so good for the studios and revenue brought in for individual games."

All of which brings us to 2026 — when Spencer departed, and Sharma was brought in to "reset" the business in his place.

While Kiln and South of Midnight may not have been big money-spinners, development studios such as Double Fine and Compulsion were simply doing what they had been told five years ago, Schreier said, when they were acquired and their projects were greenlit. Namely, that they were to make games to help fill out the Xbox Game Pass catalog and slowly keep growing its subscriber base, and that this would be enough.

Xbox Games Series Tier List

Xbox Games Series Tier List

"A lot of these studios made plenty of their own mistakes, but in a lot of ways they're being punished today for following orders," Schreier added. "For listening to what they were told a few years ago. And that is just a shame, and what is going to happen is pretty brutal. The word bloodbath has been thrown around among people I talk to who know about what's going to be happen. It's going to be bad.

"And one of the reasons Xbox studios have struggled to make great games over the past decade is this uncertainty," he concluded. "It's really hard to make great art when working under the fear of layoffs and turbulence and cancellations and shutdowns."

This week, it was alleged that Microsoft announced Ninja Theory's new game, Senua, knowing that it planned to shut the studio down because it believed the promise of a newly announced game would help draw investor interest in the developer. There is now concern that Senua may never come out.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

Recommends

item.title: Gecko Gods coming to Switch 2 on June 25 alongside ‘Forgotten Cove’ update
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2

A whole new island for players to explore.

Publisher Super Rare Originals and developer Inresin will release a Switch 2 version of puzzle platformer Gecko Gods on June 25 alongside the “Forgotten Cove” update, which will add a whole new island, the companies announced. Users who own the Switch version will be able to upgrade to the Switch 2 version at no additional cost.

Gecko Gods first launched for PlayStation 5, Switch, and PC via Steam on April 16

Here is an overview of the game, via Super Rare Originals:

About

Gecko Gods lets you take control of a curious and adorable gecko where you will climb across sunlit ruins, weave your way through lush caves, and uncover the remnants of a lost civilization. Every path you take invites discovery as you glide between islands on your gecko-sized boat, each with their own secrets to uncover.

Key Features

  • Explore Freely – Scale walls, ceilings and cliffs with the gecko’s effortless agility. Climb to the highest peaks or the deepest caves as you play with near unlimited freedom of movement.
  • A Gecko-Sized Journey – Experience a world designed for curiosity and gentle adventure, where movement itself is part of the joy. Enjoy the pitter-patter of the geckos little feet on the sand or the gentle sound of the waves as you sail through the ocean.
  • Solve Ancient Puzzles – Delve into ruins and temples filled with intuitive, environmental challenges. Solve puzzles to unlock the secrets of each island, and get hints from the critters you meet along the way.
  • Sail Between Islands – Drift across tranquil seas to discover unique landscapes, each with its own atmosphere and hidden life.


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/gecko-gods-coming-to-switch-2-on-june-25-alongside-forgotten-cove-update
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: I, God's Worst Video Game Driver, Played A Bunch Of Driving Game Demos
item.content:encoded:
You know how some people can’t picture an apple in their minds? I have a problem like that with cars in video games: Throw me in the classic position behind and slightly above a virtual vehicle, and I just lose all ability to conceptualize the entirety of a car and how it moves in space.

I smash into walls, I spin out on turns, I will never actually understand what drifting is and when I want to employ it–it’s a disaster. But still, I really like the idea of driving games, many of which involve exploring a landscape, one of my favorite things to do in games and real life. A bunch of driving games I’ve wishlisted came up as having demos during this year’s Steam Next Fest, and even as I wondered why I’d wishlisted so many driving games when I’m so shit at video game driving, I was excited to check them out.

Truck-Kun Is Supporting Me From Another World

I watched a lot of folks have fun with Truck-Kun Is Supporting Me From Another World at last week’s Tribeca Games Showcase, but I didn’t get a chance to try it myself until its Steam demo. You play as a truck driver who accidentally hits a young woman named Carissa, transporting you both to an isekai that honestly seems a lot like the regular world, except in this one you’re supposed to run down people, objects, and other cars. Doing this powers up Carissa in battles against monsters, as you try to help her get home before her big new job starts on Monday.

As you might guess from the trailer above, it’s a fast-paced, busy game. You have a time limit during each of your runs through its town, where you try to meet goals like running over a number of pedestrians, sideswiping cop cars, flying through the air, or running over specific objects. At the same time, you need to keep an eye on Carissa, shown in a pixel art bar at the bottom of your screen, and deploy her powered-up abilities to help her slash through foes. While this seems like a lot, and definitely felt like a lot as the tutorial explained it to me, in practice it all just means driving around like a lunatic and smashing buttons.

While I wasn’t totally in love with the game’s self-aware humor, I did enjoy playing a game that rewarded me for being a shitty driver instead of punishing me. It’s fun to just cause chaos; I never got tired of being encircled by armored cop cars, only to take them out with a dramatic side swipe. Truck-Kun is set to come out July 29.

Slow Roads

Slow Roads was originally a browser game, which you can still play. You just drive around a procedurally-generated landscape: the road twists and winds, you pass rivers and forests and hills, and that’s all you basically do… forever.

You can change the time of day and weather on the fly, and I liked how much this changed the mood of my drive. A sunny day made me want to go fast; dusk and dawn felt meditative; driving through the night or through stormy weather made me cautious. You can’t wreck your car or break anything–it’s just about exploring the road for as long as you feel like. 

Since this is what I like about driving games anyway, I really enjoyed the demo. I also appreciated the game’s AI disclosure, which is educational while passionately disclosing no AI at all:

No AI has been used to generate any of the code or assets used in Slow Roads; this has been a purely human endeavour from the beginning of the project in 2021. Slow Roads is a solo-developed project, built with a decade of industry experience. Assets are created first-hand where possible, with most textures hand-drawn or composed from local photography. I believe AI should have no place in artistic exploits, and it will never be used for generating any elements of Slow Roads. Please note that procedural generation is not a form of generative AI; the procedures involved are carefully-designed mathematical functions used to generate the environments according to specific rules, with directed creative intent.

The Steam version will have more roads and locations, car customization, and the ability to add your own music to the game. There’s no release date yet.

Over The Hill

I think I wishlisted Over The Hill because it reminded me of a different in-development car game I like the look of, Garbage Country. Both have these tiny, boxy cars that look so fun to smash around a bumpy virtual world.

It is indeed super fun to bump around in Over The Hill’s wilderness, even if a lot of my time was spent flipping my car over by accident or failing to get the right traction on small inclines. Unlike Slow Roads, this isn’t a game that captures the joys of easy speed; most of my progress was painstaking, as I’d tilt over rocks or get beached on trees and have to use my car’s winch to haul myself out. At one point I stumbled upon a “challenge track,” a skinny, rough trail alongside a rushing river, and I honestly couldn’t believe it when I pulled the whole thing off, given how badly I’d done on its regular roads.

In addition to the winch that I used way too often, you also have different gearing to help you progress, and you can choose either manual or automatic transmission. (I have never once in my life chosen manual transmission in a game, for the reasons that start this blog.) As you travel, you find crates that give you car customization options, as well as cabins you can fast travel between or which repair your battered car. There are a lot of other tools and unlocks that didn’t seem available in the demo, and you can also play with up to three friends.

Despite seeming to be a game literally about having a hard time driving, a situation I face under the best of conditions, I really enjoyed Over The Hill. Your blocky car and the blocky landscape remind me of Lonely Mountains, and the whole thing feels like playing with a toy. It’s easy to get yourself out of a jam by just fast traveling back to a cabin, which inspired me to really give its tracks my all. I really liked its slow, effortful pace and am excited to spend more time with the demo. Over The Hill plans to come out this year. 



https://aftermath.site/driving-games-steam-next-fest/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Here’s How I Know We Are Entering Rockstar’s End Game For Launching GTA 6
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 is launching on November 19. Some of you might roll your eyes at the seeming certainty of that statement, and understandably so. Rockstar and Take-Two have delayed the game a few times now. But, this time, I’m feeling very confident that GTA 6 is actually, for real, seriously launching as planned. Why? Because Rockstar has cmade all their social media accounts pink.

On Thursday, Rockstar Games announced that pre-orders for GTA 6 would go live on June 25. Alongside that news came the reveal of the game’s official cover art. It looks rad and has some serious GTA Vice City on PS2 vibes. And as all of this was happening, Rockstar Games went around and changed all of the profile pics and banners found on the company’s various social media accounts. Before, they featured the old classic yellow Rockstar logo.

Now, however, they all feature purple and pink palm trees with a neon-white Rockstar logo plastered on top. Why does this matter? Well, it signals to me that the company has officially entered “GTA 6 Marketing Mode.”

Historically (and there’s not that much to go on because Rockstar’s slow release schedule means they haven’t put out all that many new games in the social media era), once the company switches its icons and enters this phase of marketing for a game, it doesn’t announce a new delay. You can look back at Rockstar’s Facebook page to see that once the company swapped its icon for a Red Dead Redemption 2-themed logo in May 2018, a few months after announcing a delay, the game stuck to its October 2018 launch date.

Essentially, once Rockstar and Take-Two start showing off the cover, taking people’s money, and swapping social media icons and banners, you can be pretty damn sure that everyone involved is confident that the game is coming out as planned, barring some catastrophic event. So as long as the world doesn’t end between now and November, GTA 6 will launch as planned on November 19 on PS5 and Xbox.



https://kotaku.com/heres-how-i-know-we-are-entering-rockstars-end-game-for-launching-gta-6-2000708079

item.title: “I don’t have pressure” – molodoy on FURIA form
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to Danil ‘molodoy’ Golubenko at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major to discuss FURIA’s emotions coming into the playoffs of the event.

The Brazilian organization won four events in 2025, but has yet to taste silverware in 2026. We discuss how FURIA aim to get to the top again, and FURIA’s potential path to the Grand Final at Cologne.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Insider Gaming interviews molodoy at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

We’re here at the IEM Cologne Major playoffs. How are you feeling?

I’m good, I’m great, thank you.

Last year, you were riding high, you won four events in a row, you know, you had a great year. In 2026, it feels like it hasn’t really clicked as much. How would you contrast what’s happening in the game?

I think, first thing, the FalleN tihng and and this is, I would say, not good. But second thing, I think we are just trying to find our game.

Yeah, I think so.

Can you describe how you’re finding your game or what improvements you’ve made?

I’ll say I’m trying to change my game a little bit, and trying to help the team and trying to do more stuff. I think, yeah, that’s all.

So would that be more of a supportive role?

No, I’m not talking about support. I’m talking about all things, not only in-game, but before the game, what we do with this team and stuff like this.

  • Related: “We believe that he can be the difference-maker for us” – NiKo on playing with karrigan in the Lanxess

You guys had like two quite emotional events back to back because at IEM Rio and PGL Astana, do you have any reflections on those events?

I think in Rio and in Astana, it’s very good for us to come because it’s two, I’ll say it’s two home tournaments.

When you play this tournament, I think you’re taking a little bit of confidence in this tournament because people only support you, and I’ll say you take a little bit of motivation for yourself, and it’s very good for you and for us as well.

Do you feel like there have been moments where your performance has not been as good under that kind of pressure?

I don’t have pressure. I don’t have pressure. I’m just trying to do my best, and yeah, it’s all good.

So in terms of the team chemistry, we’ve seen yuurih not being able to find his timings, some rotations not working as well as last year. What are you working on to improve?

You know, maybe some habits, the call is not working. It’s always in Counter-Strike, sometimes maybe some gamble s**t’s working, and some stuff’s not working, and this is Counter-Strike.

It’s sometimes you can kill five guys, sometimes you can kill only one, you know, it’s Counter-Strike. Who knows what happens?

Your side of the bracket in the playoffs is maybe more favourable? How do you assess the potential matchups for you?

I cannot say it’s a kind of easy bracket. 9z plays well. We play against 9z in the first game. They beat very good teams in the PGL Astana. They beat good teams here too, and Aurora and BetBoom are good teams too. I cannot say it’s an easy bracket.

It’s a very good bracket. It’s always in a Major, you have a hard team.

How would you assess 9z’s strengths?

I’m not watching how they’re playing a lot of time, but I think I watch when they play against magic in PGL Astana. They’re playing good.

I like how they’re playing. A little bit gambly, but I like it.

In terms of, you know, your own individual form, do you feel like you’re online in the server?

I don’t know. We will see. We will see.

End of interview

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord


For other news, see the latest update on God of War Laufey and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

item.title: When Is GTA 6 Trailer 3 Releasing? (June 2026 Update)
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 is going to take the world by storm this November, and the whole gaming industry has begun to prepare for its imminent arrival. Release dates have been chopped and changed, as everyone knows it’s fruitless to go up against the titan that will be GTA 6.

That said, there’s still one major question yet to be answered: when is the third trailer for GTA 6 releasing? It’s been over a year since we last saw Vice City in all of its glory, but as we edge nearer and nearer to its release date, it’s bound to release soon, right?

Here’s everything we know about when we can expect the third trailer for GTA 6. But, before you continue, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

When Is Trailer 3 For GTA 6 Releasing?

As things stand, there is no confirmed date for the next Grand Theft Auto 6 trailer. That said, we have every reason to suspect it could be on the horizon.

Rockstar has officially confirmed that GTA 6 pre-orders will go live on June 25, finally giving fans a date they’ve been waiting years for. Alongside the announcement, the studio also unveiled the game’s official box art, leaving gameplay footage as one of the last major pieces of the marketing puzzle.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Is GTA 6 Coming Out On PC? GTA 6 PC Release, Explained

Naturally, many fans believe a third trailer could accompany the launch of pre-orders. After all, there’d be no better way to build excitement and drive sales than by showing off more of the game just before pre-orders open.

That said, this is GTA 6 we’re talking about. Rockstar could launch pre-orders tomorrow, remain completely silent until release, and the game would still sell millions of copies. Nothing says a new trailer has to arrive alongside pre-orders.

Still, if Rockstar follows the traditional marketing playbook, Trailer 3 feels closer than ever. The most likely scenario would see it arrive on June 25, ending with the announcement that pre-orders are now live.

Should anything be confirmed before the reveal, you’ll find this article aptly updated.

What Can We Expect From GTA 6 Trailer 3?

It’s all well and good hoping for another glimpse at GTA 6, but what can we realistically expect from the third trailer?

Many fans will be hoping for a closer look at gameplay. Most of what we’ve seen so far has been incredibly cinematic, highlighting the stunning views of Vice City and its surroundings, but a more grounded view of the driving, gunplay and general gameplay would go down as a treat.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everything We Learned About May 2026 Take-Two Earnings Call For GTA 6

With Rockstar confirming that the plans are to ramp up the marketing this Summer, a third trailer and pre-orders could very well be the start of a dominant Summer from Rockstar. Time will tell, but it’s starting to look like we have every reason to get excited. In the meantime, take a trip down memory lane and revisit Trailer 2!

What do you want to see from GTA 6’s third trailer? Share your wildest hopes in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following, and be sure to subscribe to our Newsletter.

  • How To Earn 2500 Free Units In Marvel Rivals—Summer Festival 2026
  • Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports—Release, Platforms, Multiplayer, File Sizes & Remaster Status

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

item.title: My Hero Academia: All’s Justice coming to Switch 2 on September 3 in Japan, September 4 worldwide
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Due out alongside free "Mini Game" content.

Publisher Bandai Namco and developer Byking will release My Hero Academia: All’s Justice for Switch 2 on September 3 in Japan and September 4 worldwide, the companies announced. The physical edition will ship on a game-key card.

My Hero Academia: All’s Justice first launched for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam on February 6.

The additional content “Mini Game (All 10 Types)” will launch for free for all versions of the game alongside the Switch 2 version’s launch.

Here is an overview of the game, via Bandai Namco:

About

SMASH through My Hero Academia‘s final story arc and triumph over your foes in spectacular 3v3 battles! Follow Deku and the journeys of other characters in the Final War between Heroes and Villains, and experience the world-shaking, climactic clash between One For All and All For One in My Hero Academia: All’s Justice!

My Hero Academia: All’s Justice is the super-powered 3D arena fighter where your favorite characters from the My Hero Academia franchise appear in their latest and most powerful forms, framed within a Refined Battle System. Furthermore, dive into a variety of game modes, including original scenario based Team-Up Mission where you’ll experience hero life as a U.A. High 1-A student in a Virtual Space!

Relive the Final War Storyline

Experience the final battle of My Hero Academia from a new perspective in My Hero Academia: All’s Justice‘s story mode. Re-live Bakugo’s fearless stand against All For One, experience Deku’s heroic struggle to bring justice and redemption to his fated foe, and more in a unique and cinematic take on the climactic clash of My Hero Academia.

The Largest My Hero Academia Roster

Heroes and Villains collide in the gigantic roster of My Hero Academia characters! Play your favorite U.A. Students, including all of Class 1-A, Pro Heroes like Mirko and Best Jeanist, and Villains such as All For One, Shigaraki and Dabi from across the full story of My Hero Academia, including their final and full-powered versions!

Unleash Your Quirks and Potential in Fast-paced Strategic Battles

Take control of your favorite U.A. Students, Pro Heroes, and Villains, and utilize their unique Quirks to pummel, blast, and trick your foes.

Unleash each character’s “Quirk” like never before by activating “Rising,” which allows attack power, movement speed, and Quirk actions all enhanced.

Mix and match your team’s Quirks to find synergies and add depth to your tactics and strategies. Chain combos while switching characters to seize victory!

A Day in the Life of a Hero

Step into the shoes of Deku and Class 1-A students in “Team-Up Mission” and tackle training exercise in a Virtual Space! Based on an original game scenario form a team with Class 1-A students as your allies and complete various missions and triumph over Villains in an exclusive map!

Furthermore, jump into “Archives Battle” which will be unlocked by completing Team-Up Mission to experience iconic battles from across the full story of My Hero Academia.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch 2 Announce Trailer

English

Japanese

item.title: Grand Theft Auto VI Pre-Order Date And Cover Art Revealed
item.content:encoded:
It's not a new gameplay trailer, but Rockstar Games has released a new promotional video for Grand Theft Auto VI, announcing the game's pre-order date and revealing its official cover art.

Pre-orders for the game on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S open on June 25, which is one week from today. The cover art is fashioned in the traditional Grand Theft Auto style: a collage of panels depicting the two main characters, Lucia and Jason, along with bank robber Raul Batista and music mogul/entrepreneur Boobie Ike. Because the game is set in a fictionalized Florida, it's fitting to see the center panel occupied by the state's unofficial mascot, an alligator. Check out the trailer and the full cover art below. 

Grand Theft Auto VI launches on November 19. You can catch up on news about the game by watching its first trailer, its second trailer, and our previews detailing the cast and six of the major areas players will explore. In related news, Rockstar announced yesterday that the PS5 and Xbox Series X/S upgrade for Grand Theft Auto V is officially free for owners of the last-gen versions. 



https://gameinformer.com/2026/06/18/grand-theft-auto-vi-pre-order-date-and-cover-art-revealed

item.title: For some reason, Riot Games is creating a Valorant 'pub' in east London hosted by a British takeaway guy who likes to yell "bosh!"
item.content:encoded:
Ever watched a Big John video? He's a big guy from London who films himself eating food that'd probably floor me. I watched one video where he ordered three pies and two scoops of mash potato and some swamp-like sauce. Proper hearty food he calls it, then he said "bosh", and apparently he says bosh a lot.

Video game mega-developer Riot Games has inexplicably hired Big John to play landlord in an East London pub for a weekend - 19th to 21st June - that it'll deck out with Valorant pint glasses and beer-tap labels, and where you can watch the ongoing Valorant Masters event (also hosted in London) for free. The pints won't be free, though.

The idea is to capture the "quintessential British matchday pub" experience, according to a press release, so expect lots of beer and pies and shouting and maybe some tears of emotion. Though I'm not entirely sure a Valorant crowd will behave in the same way as a football one. I cringe at the thought of it, actually, but who am I to judge?

The pub in question is The Phoenix Arms in Hackney Bridge, and it'll be decked out with Valorant team flags, memorabilia, and custom pub signage. There'll be live match screenings and of course there'll be Big John. Sadly it remains unconfirmed as to whether he'll be serving - or indeed eating - any food.

The Valorant Masters itself is taking place within the Copper Box Arena up until 21st June. The arena was first built for the British Olympics back in 2012. It's a popular location for tier-one esports events, with Riot Games using the venue for a League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational.

During this Masters event, a new map reveal for Valorant will occur. The game introduced a Croatian character called Miks earlier this year, a music-minded controller able to use sonic abilities to keep the opposing team playing to his tempo (sorry).

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/valorant-masters-pub-big-john-hackney-london
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Grand Theft Auto VI preorders live from June 25th
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar Games has announced that the Grand Theft Auto VI pre-orders will be available from June 25th on PS5 & Xbox Series XS. Rockstar Games also revealed the official cover art for Grand Theft Auto VI, which you can see below. At the moment, details around pricing has not been confirmed by Rockstar and we probably won’t know the price until next week.

GTA 6 had previously been set for launch on 26th May 2026, and while this was the first firm date that we had for launch from Take Two and Rockstar, it had come after the better part of a year of Take Two execs assuring that the game was going to launch in 2025. However it was GTA 6 delayed again to November 2026, with the confirmed release date now being November 19th.

Grand Theft Auto VI will take us all back to Vice City for Rockstar’s latest crime story, featuring Lucia, the series’ first female protagonist, who’s coming fresh out of prison, and Jason, who works with the local drugrunners after quitting the army.

The trailers have shown off the modern day setting in the fictionalised state of Leonida, with a handful of things like social media influence and excesses feeding into the atmosphere, though that won’t stop there being plenty of iconic classic cars and inspirations from the breadth of American culture in the South East of the country and Florida in particular. From packed beaches to going across the swamps on hover craft, and partying at the tops of skyscrapers, there’s also jokes thrown into the trailer about battling crocodiles in back garden swimming pools, “Floridaman” antics – Leonidaman in the game – and plenty more.

Source: Rockstar Games



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/18/grand-theft-auto-vi-preorders-live-from-june-25th/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rockstar Confirms That Grand Theft Auto 6 Pre-Orders Kick Off on June 25 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S|X
item.content:encoded:
In pure Rockstar Games style, the studio has suddenly announced that pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto 6 will open on June 25 for Sony's PlayStation 5 and Microsoft's Xbox Series S and X. The studio also unveiled the official cover art, which is very much in line with previous GTA covers.

Rockstar Games’ Grand Theft Auto 6 is shaping up to be the biggest entertainment release ever, with certain analysts suggesting it could sell as many as 46 million units on day one.

The game was first officially revealed on December 4, 2023, and the trailer immediately broke records, becoming the most-viewed non-music video on YouTube in its first 12 hours. Since then, Rockstar has delayed the game twice, first pushing it to May 26, 2026 and then again to November 19, 2026, the current launch date, with Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick still saying in February that the November date remained on track.

As with past GTA games, the launch will begin on consoles, specifically PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, while a PC version has not been officially confirmed. A PC release later in 2027 or 2028 remains plausible based on Rockstar's usual release pattern.

The setting returns to Vice City, now part of the broader state of Leonida, and the game will feature two playable protagonists, Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos. Rockstar’s trailers and website suggest a satirical, modern Florida filled with social media excess, crime, and a larger, denser open world than in GTA 5.

For more about Grand Theft Auto 6, check out our detailed info roundup page.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/gta-6-pre-orders-june-25-ps5-xbox-series-xs/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Start Next Week As Rockstar Reveals The Game’s Official Cover Art
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar Games has announced that starting next week, you’ll be able to pre-order Grand Theft Auto 6. And while it didn’t release a new trailer alongside this news, it did reveal the official cover for the game.

On June 18, Rockstar Games confirmed that you’ll be able to pre-order GTA 6 on digital storefronts and at “select retailers” starting on June 25. Yes, finally, GTA fans will be able to actually give Rockstar money for the highly anticipated next entry in the Grand Theft Auto series. I imagine this makes a lot of GTA fans and Take-Two execs very happy. No other info was revealed about whether there will be different editions. And more importantly, we still don’t know how much GTA 6 will cost. But we’ll find out the price next week.

Pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto VI will officially begin on June 25 on digital storefronts and at other select retailers.

Check out the official cover art, also available as downloadable artwork at https://t.co/XPwC8URCQ4 pic.twitter.com/pRVXk4eyDQ

— Rockstar Games (@RockstarGames) June 18, 2026

Besides the news that GTA 6 pre-orders start soon, Rockstar also revealed the official cover art for Grand Theft Auto 6. Here’s a nice high-res image of it for you to look over cloesly:

Ohhh, very pretty and colorful. I especially like the alligator hanging out on the cover. Good for him. This cover has some big GTA: Vice City on PS2 vibes, and that ain’t a bad thing in my book at all. Mostly, I’m just happy that all of the game media will have a new image to use when writing about GTA 6 between now and the game’s release in November. Hopefully, none of those stories involves a delay.

If Rockstar and Take-Two are taking pre-order dollars and showing off the cover, it feels like we are actually getting this game in 2026. Wowza. Exciting stuff. And if it does get delayed out of November, well, that’s going to be really awkward for the rest of the industry that all ran away and hid in October and September.

Grand Theft Auto 6 is launching on November 19 on PS5 and Xbox. Pre-orders start on June 25.



https://kotaku.com/gta-6-pre-orders-start-next-week-as-rockstar-reveals-the-games-official-cover-art-2000708063
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Burger Motorsports to Pause Operations for GTA 6 Release Day
item.content:encoded:
Burger Motorsports will temporarily pause operations on November 19, 2026, citing employee scheduling conflicts tied to GTA 6’s launch.

The release of Grand Theft Auto 6 continues to be anticipated as a major cultural event. The game will launch on November 19, as Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick has confirmed several times. In anticipation of employees being too preoccupied to come to work, one business has already decided to temporarily pause operations that day.

The business in question is a burger joint (in name only), but before getting into the meat of the matter, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android.  

Burger Motorsports Closes for GTA 6 Amid Staff Conflicts

Burger Motorsports is the business that made this decision. In an internal company memo shared on the brand’s social media pages, the company announced that it made this decision after reviewing multiple employee scheduling conflicts and realizing that everything might be tied to the game’s release

“Normal business operations are expected to resume once employees have completed their initial exploration, finished at least one mission, and returned to reality. We appreciate your patience and understanding during this unprecedented cultural event,” the closing of the memo reads.

  • More GTA 6 News: GTA 6 Pre-Order Hype Causes Huge Malware Concern

Rockstar Games recently announced that pre-orders for the game will be available starting June 25. This adds further confirmation that the release date is now firmly established. The game was initially scheduled for release in late 2025, then moved to May of this year, and has since been postponed to November.

How do you feel about this move by Burger Motorsports? Are you excited for the impending pre-orders for GTA 6? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server.

For even more news on Grand Theft Auto from Insider Gaming, check out how much money GTA 5 is still bringing in. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Genshin Impact Version Luna VIII update ‘Song of the Welkin Moon: Scherzo – Sunny Summer Fontinalia’ launches July 1
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Smartphone
  • Xbox Series

Reunite with Sandrone and embark on an adventure to the moon!

The Version Luna VIII update for Genshin Impact, dubbed “Song of the Welkin Moon: Scherzo – Sunny Summer Fontinalia,” will launch on July 1, developer miHoYo announced.

Here is an overview of the update, via miHoYo:

In this update, players will reunite with Sandrone, who makes an unexpected return after sacrificing herself in the fierce battle of Nod-Krai. Meanwhile, beyond the skies of Teyvat, the Frost Moon will become available as a permanent new area, inviting players to explore its vast and breathtaking landscapes.

Sandrone, the Seventh of the Fatui Harbingers, makes an unexpected return in this version as a 5-star Cryo Claymore wielder. After a tense first encounter, players witnessed her selfless sacrifice in the fierce battle against The Doctor, helping Columbina and the Traveler secure victory. Now that she has returned, the truth behind her resurrection is set to be revealed.

Arriving alongside Sandrone is Stellar-Conduct, a brand-new Cryo-Electro reaction. When triggered by certain Cryo and Electro characters, the surrounding area will briefly transform into a Polestar Field. This field can store excess Cryo and Electro energy and convert it into buffs, increasing the Stellar-Conduct DMG, as well as Cryo DMG and Electro DMG dealt by all characters within its range, while also reducing opponents’ Physical RES. Certain existing Cryo and Electro characters can also receive unique enhancements while within the field.

In combat, Sandrone can increase the entire party’s Stellar-Conduct DMG through the Polestar Field. She commands Fagio, a fully autonomous construct that deals Stellar-Conduct DMG with each Charged Attack and accumulates Decoding Power. Once Fagio reaches its firing limit, its attack intervals will increase. By using her Elemental Skill, Sandrone can maneuver through the Polestar Field while firing at opponents, reducing Decoding Power to prevent Fagio from overloading. Her Elemental Burst bombards opponents with artillery fire, and beam DMG dealt within the Polestar Field will be considered Stellar-Conduct DMG.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Sandrone will make her debut in the first half of the Version Luna VIII Character Event Wishes alongside Citlali’s rerun, while the second half will feature the return of Columbina and Raiden Shogun.

Players will board a spacecraft and journey beyond the skies of Teyvat, finally setting foot on the moon, where a vast new region awaits exploration. The moon is home to many breathtaking sights unlike anything seen in Teyvat, including a luminous yet treacherous lunar sea and a space station suspended in outer space, from which players can gaze upon Teyvat as it quietly rotates in the distance. In addition, majestic ancient ruins await exploration, revealing more hidden stories tied to the ancient dragon race. A mysterious ancient power permeates the lunar surface, allowing players to traverse various types of terrain and manipulate the local gravity. To further improve the exploration experience, both the moon and Teyvat maps will receive a new layered display feature that shows the player’s current level in real time.

The Fontinalia Film Festival sets sail aboard the Wingalet in Fontaine, where Charlotte, Citlali, and many familiar faces gather to celebrate a midsummer water festival. Sandrone will also come aboard for the journey, while Columbina arrives with the film crew for a shoot. The event story will become permanent. Players can help manage the ship’s aquarium and attract visitors to join the festivities. After completing the event, players can invite Charlotte to their party for free and obtain a brand-new outfit for her. In addition, Citlali’s new outfit will be available at a limited-time discount. Players can also take part in a dance challenge back in Natlan, explore new pieces in the Repertoire of Myriad Melodies, and try out new cards in Genius Invokation TCG.

In this update, Miliastra Wonderland will receive a brand-new summer-themed lobby and summer-themed outfits. By completing fun activities, players can also claim free rewards, including Prismatic Crystals and an elegant 4-star set. For Craftspeople, the Editor will receive several practical upgrades, along with a selection of new environment assets that open up more possibilities for interactive gameplay and diverse visual design.

Genshin Impact is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PC via client and Epic Games Store, iOS via the App Store, and Android via Google Play.

Watch a new set of trailers below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

item.title: Rockstar reveals the Grand Theft Auto 6 box art, which is quite nice I suppose, and pre-order dates for the game
item.content:encoded:
Crocodile! Flamingo! Lamborghini!

Rockstar Games has revealed the box art for Grand Theft Auto 6, which looks a lot like you'd expect it to look, and it's revealed pre-order dates for the new game.

The box art features the familiar GTA box design of the game-name surrounded by panels of artwork. Here we see a yellow Lamborghini-like vehicle, a speed boat, a crocodile, a helicopter, someone doing an irresponsible wheelie on a sports motorbike, someone holding a machine gun, perhaps irresponsibly, someone dressed in a bikini, someone wearing lots of bling, and of course the game's dual heroes, Lucia and Jason.

There's also a flamingo because we're in Vice City for this adventure and I assume Flamingos hang out there, or perhaps they just fit with the pink and purple aesthetic.

PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto 6 begin 25th June, meanwhile. I don't expect there'll be any shortage of copies but who knows? It's reassuring to see confidence in the 19th November release date all the same.

It's an unexpected announcement that comes a day after an employment tribunal ruled against Rockstar Games in an ongoing UK legal battle related to lay-offs and allegations of union busting.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/rockstar-grand-theft-auto-6-gta-box-art
